title: LUTS/BPH
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

examination.text: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped adult male LUTS history, abdominal or bladder or rectal examination, urinalysis, qualified PSA or creatinine, conservative-advice, referral, follow-up, and combined-history fields.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management

examination.text: Supports specialist history and examination, urinary flow, post-void residual, and recommendation-qualified imaging or cystoscopy context.
examination.rationale: Supports mapped urinary-flow and post-void-residual documentation in specialist assessment.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports specialist history and examination, urinary flow, post-void residual, and recommendation-qualified imaging or cystoscopy context.

source.title: Lower urinary tract symptoms in men: management

investigations.text: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.
investigations.rationale: Supports mapped acute-retention prompts without mapping catheter, medicine, or surgery instructions.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

source_limitations: The workflow does not encode adult male eligibility, symptom severity, specialist setting, renal-impairment suspicion, cancer-risk, or retention qualifiers.

Fever, neurological examination, new onset after age 50, broad vitals, previous records, generic clinician plan, medication plan, fixed follow-up intervals, PRN wording, and automatic BPH diagnosis remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, PSA interpretation, medicine selection or dose, catheter care, surgery, referral urgency, and exact follow-up timing remain clinician responsibilities.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
